normative_source.standard_name: OWASP Mobile Application Security Verification Standard
normative_source.requirement_title: The app prevents leakage of sensitive data.
normative_source.normative_excerpt: Per OWASP MASVS v2.1.0 §MASVS-STORAGE-2 (Description): There are cases when sensitive data is unintentionally stored or exposed to publicly accessible locations; typically as a side-effect of using certain APIs, system capabilities such as backups or logs. This control covers this kind of unintentional leaks where the developer actually has a way to prevent it.
normative_source.normative_summary: Sensitive data can leak unintentionally through platform features the developer didn't explicitly use for storage: system backups, app logs, keyboard caches, system clipboard, auto-generated screenshots in app switchers, crash reports, analytics SDKs, and IPC mechanisms. MASVS-STORAGE-2 differs from MASVS-STORAGE-1: STORAGE-1 covers data the app intentionally stores; STORAGE-2 covers leaks via side effects. The control is bounded by what the developer can actually prevent — the standard explicitly scopes it to preventable leaks.

source_rights.license_notes: Source license recorded from the downloaded OWASP MASVS v2.1.0 PDF artifact. Live website may differ; this corpus pins the artifact license. Verbatim excerpts include attribution per CC-BY-SA-4.0 §3.

applicability.human_note: Applies to every Tier 3+ app. Per mapping file v1.2, MASVS-STORAGE-2 is authoritatively mapped to two Constitution v0.5 traces: SEC-PILLAR-2-SENSITIVE-DATA-LEAKAGE-PREVENTION (primary, covering the full preventable-leakage surface — backups, logs, crash reports, analytics, clipboard, screenshots, IPC payloads, exported files) and SEC-PILLAR-2-KEYBOARD-CACHE (supporting, covering the specific keyboard-cache vector at Tier 3+). Tier_floor is 2, matching the primary trace's applicable tier range. APPLICABILITY TODO: Sequoia v2.6 raises data-sensitivity questions conversationally during blueprinting but the blueprint schema does not yet land a structured `handles_sensitive_data` boolean or `sensitive_data_classes[]` array that corpus conditions can mechanically check. When that field lands (blueprint schema patch + Scribe extraction + PIPELINE_LANGUAGE §2.5 update), this applicability should migrate to use it directly — that would extend STORAGE-2 coverage below Tier 3 honestly.

intrinsic_interpretation.mapping_rationale: Mapping file v1.1 authoritatively maps SEC-PILLAR-2-KEYBOARD-CACHE to MASVS-STORAGE-2 with the canonical rationale: 'MASVS-STORAGE-2 requires that sensitive data not be leaked through unintended storage mechanisms. Keyboard caches are a documented unintended storage vector for credentials and PII; disabling them on sensitive fields directly addresses this requirement.' The mapping is partial because MASVS-STORAGE-2's leakage surface is broad — beyond keyboard caches, it covers backups, logs, screenshots, clipboard, crash reports, analytics SDK exfiltration, and IPC mechanisms. The keyboard-cache trace directly covers one important vector; the other vectors are evaluated by file_standards_auditor (per-file scanning for logged-sensitive-data and improper backup config) and the Reaper specialists reaper-mobile (mobile platform attack surface) and reaper-privacy (PII handling correctness). Mapping file v1.2 may add additional Constitution traces (e.g., backup-exclusion configuration, screen protection scoped to STORAGE-2's leakage frame) as authoritatively mapped to STORAGE-2; until then, this corpus relies on the keyboard-cache trace plus auditor judgment for the long tail.

expected_evidence_patterns: iOS: NSURLIsExcludedFromBackupKey set on files containing sensitive data; Info.plist NSExcludedFromBackup configured for sensitive directories
Android: backup_rules.xml explicitly excludes sensitive directories; android:fullBackupContent declared; android:dataExtractionRules declared for Android 12+

Tier 3+ sensitive input fields disable keyboard cache per SEC-PILLAR-2-KEYBOARD-CACHE: iOS textContentType set appropriately (.password, .creditCardNumber, .oneTimeCode) and autocorrectionType = .no; Android android:inputType includes textNoSuggestions or textPassword/textVisiblePassword

Screenshot protection on sensitive screens: iOS application willResignActiveNotification → cover-view overlay or window.isHidden = true; Android getWindow().setFlags(FLAG_SECURE, FLAG_SECURE) on sensitive Activities/Fragments
Logging guards: sensitive values redacted or omitted from NSLog/Log.d/print/console.log calls; production logging gated on DEBUG-only build flags

Clipboard discipline: when sensitive data is copied to clipboard (passwords, OTPs), clipboard cleared after time-bounded interval (iOS UIPasteboard.expirationDate, Android ClipboardManager + delayed clear)
failure_indicators: Sensitive data values present in NSLog/Log.d/print/console.log/os_log calls in production-reachable code paths
android:allowBackup="true" without backup_rules.xml exclusion rules for sensitive directories (Android default behavior backs up everything)
Sensitive input fields (passwords, credit cards, government IDs, OTP codes) without keyboard cache disabling where required by Constitution Pillar 2 for the declared tier
Sensitive screens without screenshot protection where the Constitution requires it for the declared tier and data surface
Crash reporting SDKs (Crashlytics, Sentry, Bugsnag) configured without explicit PII scrubbing rules
Sensitive data passed through IPC mechanisms (iOS UIActivityViewController, Android Intent extras, Content Providers) without explicit redaction
false_positive_traps: Logging non-sensitive operational data (cache hit rates, screen-load timings, API endpoint identifiers) is acceptable
Backup of non-sensitive data (user preferences, app theme, language) is acceptable — only sensitive data triggers backup-exclusion requirements
Test fixtures (paths containing /test/, /tests/, /__tests__/, /spec/ OR functions marked @Test, @Ignore, @Disabled) using verbose logging are not production violations
Screenshot protection is not required on non-sensitive screens (settings, about, help, marketing surfaces)
Clipboard usage for non-sensitive data (copy-link, copy-message-text) does not require time-bounded clearing

allowed_claims: disables keyboard cache on sensitive fields per Constitution Pillar 2 (SEC-PILLAR-2-KEYBOARD-CACHE, Tier 3+) as evidence toward MASVS-STORAGE-2 coverage
provides evidence toward MASVS-STORAGE-2 coverage via keyboard-cache control plus auditor evaluation of additional leakage vectors (backup, screenshot, logging)
claim_boundaries.claim_boundary_notes: Standard-level baseline (from manifest.json.standard_claim_policy) covers OWASP-wide forbidden claims. MASVS-STORAGE-2 is bounded by the source standard itself to leaks 'the developer actually has a way to prevent' — claims about preventing OS-level or hardware-level leaks (kernel memory dumps, hardware side channels) are out of scope and should not be inferred. The corpus does not certify completeness against every possible leakage vector; the External Standards Mapping Auditor evaluates evidence against the listed verification_guidance patterns. Only SEC-PILLAR-2-KEYBOARD-CACHE is authoritatively mapped to MASVS-STORAGE-2 in mapping file v1.1; claims about additional Constitution traces covering STORAGE-2's broader leakage surface should reference the auditor evaluation chain, not unverified trace mappings. Tier routing note: file_standards_auditor and external_standards_mapping_auditor are tier-agnostic. Reaper specialist invocation follows the Reaper Conclave tier matrix; the listed Reaper auditors consume this requirement when invoked for the bundle's tier and surface.
